sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Atlético Madrid are in strong form, having won 4 of their last 5 matches, including a convincing 3-0 victory over Sevilla. Levante UD, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 5 games, managing only 2 draws and suffering 3 losses, including a recent 1-2 defeat to Celta Vigo.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, Atlético Madrid secured a 2-0 victory in 2023, while Levante UD managed a surprising 1-0 win in 2022. Historically, matches between these sides tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Atlético Madrid, sitting 4th with 22 points, have been defensively solid, conceding the fewest goals in the league (10), while scoring 21. Levante UD, languishing in 17th with 9 points, have scored 15 but conceded 20, showing defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Julián Álvarez has been pivotal for Atlético Madrid with 7 goals this season, ranking 2nd in the league, while Marcos Llorente historically performs well against Levante. For Levante, Etta Eyong is a threat with 6 goals, but their lack of in-form players could hinder them.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook
sections.text: Atlético Madrid’s high possession (53%) and ability to create big chances (38, 3rd in league) suggest they will dominate the game. Levante’s poor defensive stats and inability to keep clean sheets (none in last 5 matches) make them vulnerable.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Atlético Madrid’s superior form, league position, and home advantage at Riyadh Air Metropolitano, combined with Sofascore’s 83% probability of a home win, they are clear favorites. Levante’s struggles and lack of consistency make an upset unlikely.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Atlético Madrid and Levante UD ended in a 1-1 stalemate at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano. Both teams showed moments of quality, with early attacking intent resulting in goals from Adrián de la Fuente for Levante and a response from Manuel Sánchez for Atlético Madrid. Despite the level scoreline, the home side dominated possession and created more opportunities, while Levante relied on counter-attacking moments to stay competitive.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid showcased their dominance with a significant share of possession and a high number of entries into the final third and penalty area. Their ability to create big chances was evident, though they missed a key opportunity to take the lead. Levante, on the other hand, were far less involved in attack, focusing on a defensive structure but managing to capitalize on a rare chance to score. Their aerial strength was notable, winning a higher percentage of duels in the air compared to Atlético, which could be a key factor in set-piece situations.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: From the opening whistle, Atlético Madrid took control with consistent attacking pressure, showing multiple spells of dominance throughout the first half. Their momentum was particularly strong in the early stages and again towards the latter part of the half, where they pushed hard for a lead. Levante had fewer moments of threat, but a brief shift in momentum around the midway point of the half allowed them to level the score. Overall, the home side dictated the tempo for the majority of the half, with only sporadic resistance from the visitors.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and discipline
halftimeSections.text: For Atlético Madrid, players like Alejandro Baena and Pablo Barrios stood out with their involvement in creating chances and taking shots on target, though they couldn’t convert their opportunities. Levante’s Manuel Sánchez was clinical in scoring, while their goalkeeper Mathew Ryan earned high praise for keeping the scoreline level. Discipline-wise, Levante picked up a yellow card, which could influence their defensive approach in the second half, while Atlético remained unbooked and can afford to push with more aggression.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead, Atlético Madrid are likely to continue their high-pressing game and capitalize on their superior possession to create more scoring chances. Levante will need to shore up their defense and rely on quick transitions to catch the hosts off guard. Given the home side’s expected goals and missed big chance in the first half, they are in a strong position to edge ahead if they maintain their intensity. The second half could see Atlético pulling away if Levante fail to adapt to the pressure.
